|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/I_Didn%27t_Know_What_Time_It_Was "Special:EditPage/I Didn't Know What Time It Was") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["I Didn't Know What Time It Was"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22I+Didn%27t+Know+What+Time+It+Was%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22I+Didn%27t+Know+What+Time+It+Was%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22I+Didn%27t+Know+What+Time+It+Was%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22I+Didn%27t+Know+What+Time+It+Was%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22I+Didn%27t+Know+What+Time+It+Was%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22I+Didn%27t+Know+What+Time+It+Was%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(October 2018)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

1939 song by Richard Kollmar

| "I Didn't Know What Time It Was" | |
| --- | --- |
| [Song](/wiki/Song "Song") by [Richard Kollmar](/wiki/Richard_Kollmar "Richard Kollmar") | |
| Released | 1939 (1939) |
| [Genre](/wiki/Music_genre "Music genre") | Pop |
| [Composer](/wiki/Composer "Composer") | [Richard Rodgers](/wiki/Richard_Rodgers "Richard Rodgers") |
| [Lyricist](/wiki/Lyricist "Lyricist") | [Lorenz Hart](/wiki/Lorenz_Hart "Lorenz Hart") |

"**I Didn't Know What Time It Was**" is a [popular](/wiki/Popular_music "Popular music") song composed by [Richard Rodgers](/wiki/Richard_Rodgers "Richard Rodgers") with lyrics by [Lorenz Hart](/wiki/Lorenz_Hart "Lorenz Hart") for the 1939 musical *[Too Many Girls](/wiki/Too_Many_Girls_(musical) "Too Many Girls (musical)")*.
Introduced by [Richard Kollmar](/wiki/Richard_Kollmar "Richard Kollmar") and Marcy Westcott in the stage musical,[[1]](#cite_note-1) early hit versions were recorded by [Benny Goodman](/wiki/Benny_Goodman "Benny Goodman") (vocal by [Louise Tobin](/wiki/Louise_Tobin "Louise Tobin")) and by [Jimmy Dorsey](/wiki/Jimmy_Dorsey "Jimmy Dorsey") (vocal by [Bob Eberly](/wiki/Bob_Eberly "Bob Eberly")).[[2]](#cite_note-2)  
It was then performed by [Trudy Erwin](/wiki/Trudy_Erwin "Trudy Erwin") (dubbing for [Lucille Ball](/wiki/Lucille_Ball "Lucille Ball")) and [Richard Carlson](/wiki/Richard_Carlson_(actor) "Richard Carlson (actor)") in [the 1940 film adaptation](/wiki/Too_Many_Girls_(film) "Too Many Girls (film)") produced by [RKO](/wiki/RKO "RKO").[[3]](#cite_note-3) The song was later interpolated into the score of the 1957 film *[Pal Joey](/wiki/Pal_Joey_(film) "Pal Joey (film)"),* sung by [Frank Sinatra](/wiki/Frank_Sinatra "Frank Sinatra"), and has become a jazz standard.

## Recordings

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=I_Didn%27t_Know_What_Time_It_Was&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Recordings")]

* [Artie Shaw](/wiki/Artie_Shaw "Artie Shaw") - Shadows / I Didn't Know What Time It Was (1939)[[4]](#cite_note-standards-4)
* [Charlie Parker](/wiki/Charlie_Parker "Charlie Parker") – *[Charlie Parker with Strings](/wiki/Charlie_Parker_with_Strings "Charlie Parker with Strings")* (1949)[[4]](#cite_note-standards-4)
* [Peggy Lee](/wiki/Peggy_Lee "Peggy Lee") – *[Black Coffee](/wiki/Black_Coffee_(Peggy_Lee_album) "Black Coffee (Peggy Lee album)")* (1953)[[4]](#cite_note-standards-4)
* [Louis Jordan](/wiki/Louis_Jordan "Louis Jordan") (1954)
* [Ella Fitzgerald](/wiki/Ella_Fitzgerald "Ella Fitzgerald") - *[Ella Fitzgerald Sings the Rodgers and Hart Songbook](/wiki/Ella_Fitzgerald_Sings_the_Rodgers_and_Hart_Songbook "Ella Fitzgerald Sings the Rodgers and Hart Songbook")* (1956)
* [Stan Getz](/wiki/Stan_Getz "Stan Getz") and [Gerry Mulligan](/wiki/Gerry_Mulligan "Gerry Mulligan") – *[Gerry Mulligan Meets Stan Getz](/wiki/Gerry_Mulligan_Meets_Stan_Getz "Gerry Mulligan Meets Stan Getz")* (1957)[[4]](#cite_note-standards-4)
* [Billie Holiday](/wiki/Billie_Holiday "Billie Holiday") – *[Songs for Distingué Lovers](/wiki/Songs_for_Distingu%C3%A9_Lovers "Songs for Distingué Lovers")* (1957)
* [Sonny Clark](/wiki/Sonny_Clark "Sonny Clark") - *[Sonny Clark Trio](/wiki/Sonny_Clark_Trio "Sonny Clark Trio")* (1957)[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* [Bobby Darin](/wiki/Bobby_Darin "Bobby Darin") - *[Love Swings](/wiki/Love_Swings "Love Swings")* (1958)
* [Benny Golson](/wiki/Benny_Golson "Benny Golson") with [Curtis Fuller](/wiki/Curtis_Fuller "Curtis Fuller") – *[Groovin' with Golson](/wiki/Groovin%27_with_Golson "Groovin' with Golson")* (1959)[[4]](#cite_note-standards-4)
* [Barry Harris](/wiki/Barry_Harris "Barry Harris") – *[Listen to Barry Harris](/wiki/Listen_to_Barry_Harris "Listen to Barry Harris")* (1961)[[4]](#cite_note-standards-4)
* [Art Blakey](/wiki/Art_Blakey "Art Blakey") with [Wayne Shorter](/wiki/Wayne_Shorter "Wayne Shorter") – *Ugetsu* (1963)[[4]](#cite_note-standards-4)
* [Timi Yuro](/wiki/Timi_Yuro "Timi Yuro") - *[The Amazing Timi Yuro](/w/index.php?title=The_Amazing_Timi_Yuro&action=edit&redlink=1 "The Amazing Timi Yuro (page does not exist)")* (1964)
* The Crampton Sisters - their 1964 revival for the DCP label was a Hot 100 entry, peaking at #92.[[6]](#cite_note-6)
* [McCoy Tyner](/wiki/McCoy_Tyner "McCoy Tyner") with [Bobby Hutcherson](/wiki/Bobby_Hutcherson "Bobby Hutcherson") – *[Time for Tyner](/wiki/Time_for_Tyner "Time for Tyner")* (1968)[[4]](#cite_note-standards-4)
* [Ray Charles](/wiki/Ray_Charles "Ray Charles") - #74 in [Canada](/wiki/RPM_(magazine) "RPM (magazine)"), March 17, 1969[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* [Betty Carter](/wiki/Betty_Carter "Betty Carter") – *[Betty Carter at the Village Vanguard](/wiki/Betty_Carter_at_the_Village_Vanguard "Betty Carter at the Village Vanguard")* (1970)[[4]](#cite_note-standards-4)
* [David "Fathead" Newman](/wiki/David_%22Fathead%22_Newman "David \"Fathead\" Newman") – *[Captain Buckles](/wiki/Captain_Buckles "Captain Buckles")* (1971)
* [Shirley Horn](/wiki/Shirley_Horn "Shirley Horn") – *All of Me* (1987)
* [Cassandra Wilson](/wiki/Cassandra_Wilson "Cassandra Wilson") – *[Blue Skies](/wiki/Blue_Skies_(Cassandra_Wilson_album) "Blue Skies (Cassandra Wilson album)")* (1988)[[4]](#cite_note-standards-4)
* [Chick Corea](/wiki/Chick_Corea "Chick Corea") – *[Expressions](/wiki/Expressions_(Chick_Corea_album) "Expressions (Chick Corea album)")* (1994)[[4]](#cite_note-standards-4)
* [Brad Mehldau](/wiki/Brad_Mehldau "Brad Mehldau") – *[The Art of the Trio Vol. 1](/wiki/The_Art_of_the_Trio_Volume_One "The Art of the Trio Volume One")* (1997)[[4]](#cite_note-standards-4)
* [Richard Wyands Trio](/w/index.php?title=Richard_Wyands_Trio&action=edit&redlink=1 "Richard Wyands Trio (page does not exist)") - *[Lady of the Lavender Mist](/w/index.php?title=Lady_of_the_Lavender_Mist&action=edit&redlink=1 "Lady of the Lavender Mist (page does not exist)")* (1998)
* [Cécile McLorin Salvant](/wiki/C%C3%A9cile_McLorin_Salvant "Cécile McLorin Salvant") – *WomanChild* (2013)
* [Sue Raney](/wiki/Sue_Raney "Sue Raney") - *Late in Life* (2015)
* [Barbra Streisand](/wiki/Barbra_Streisand "Barbra Streisand") – *[The Music...The Mem'ries...The Magic!](/wiki/The_Music...The_Mem%27ries...The_Magic! "The Music...The Mem'ries...The Magic!")* (2017)

Apart from those listed, there are hundreds more versions of the song.[[8]](#cite_note-8)
